weaknesses: Limited stated merchandising or assortment domain experience
No explicit Power BI & Sigma combination in retail context
MBA not indicated (not current or recent MBA)
improvement_bullets: Developed automated reporting frameworks for merchandising and product teams, effectively translating raw sales data into actionable promotional and assortment insights to drive inventory efficiency.
Utilized Snowflake and SQL to analyze large retail datasets, uncovering key trends in consumer purchasing behavior and merchandising performance that informed regional inventory planning.
Built interactive self-service analytics dashboards in Power BI, enabling retail stakeholders to monitor promotional effectiveness and assortment KPIs in real-time.

Applied data-driven segmentation to optimize retail and eCommerce promotional calendars, directly contributing to a 300% increase in customer acquisition through targeted performance marketing initiatives.
Partnered with cross-functional teams to validate reporting accuracy for retail metrics, ensuring high data integrity for executive-ready summaries and strategic merchandising decisions.
cover_letter: Dear Hiring Manager, I am excited to submit my application for the MBA Consultant, Merchandising Analytics & Reporting opportunity with 1-800-FLOWERS.COM, INC. I am interested in this opportunity because of the company’s reputation as a leader in the floral and gourmet gift space, and my desire to apply rigorous data-driven methodologies to complex retail and eCommerce business challenges. What interests me about working for 1-800-FLOWERS.COM, INC. is its innovative approach to omni-channel customer engagement; this excites me because my background is deeply rooted in using data to solve real-world operational and merchandising puzzles. I believe my advanced proficiency in SQL and Snowflake, combined with my experience in building scalable dashboarding solutions, would make me a strong candidate for this role. During my professional career, I have approached business problems by bridging the gap between technical data engineering and strategic decision-making. In fact, at Shine Masters Cleaning Services, I identified that manual reporting was causing significant bottlenecks; I translated these complex performance data points into interactive Power BI dashboards and automated the underlying data workflows. The result was a 40% reduction in data processing time and a 300% increase in new customer acquisition through data-informed marketing initiatives—an impact that directly parallels the type of merchandising and promotional analysis required at 1-800-FLOWERS.COM. Ultimately, I know that the MBA Consultant, Merchandising Analytics & Reporting role at 1-800-FLOWERS.COM, INC. would allow me to bring my expertise in ETL pipelines and data visualization to your merchandising and planning initiatives. Additionally, my role as a Data Analytics Fellow at COOP Careers enabled me to develop my cross-functional collaboration and data storytelling skills. I led a 250K+ row analysis on demographic patterns, where I designed an interactive Tableau dashboard and delivered a presentation to stakeholders to support actionable city safety strategies. This project resulted in a clear, accessible reporting framework that allowed non-technical leadership to make informed, data-backed decisions. I’m excited by the opportunity to use my background in large-scale data analysis in working on merchandising performance and assortment optimization at 1-800-FLOWERS.COM. I know that my technical versatility and analytical mindset align with what it takes to succeed in the MBA Consultant, Merchandising Analytics & Reporting role. I would love the opportunity to discuss the role in more detail. Thank you for your time and consideration. I look forward to hearing from you soon. Warm regards, Justin Miller
